answers.answer: The test registers 'Ada Lovelace' and then 'ADA LOVELACE' with the same email and asserts the ledger holds two customers, demonstrating SPEC R7: names differing only by case are distinct customers. Verified passing (16/16 suite green).
The test captures 9.00 on ORD-1, records one 5.00 refund, then asserts a second 5.00 refund raises RefundError because the 10.00 aggregate would exceed the captured 9.00 (SPEC R6). Verified passing.

The test submits an order with claimed_total=999.99 but line items of 2 x 4.50 and asserts the recorded entry total is 9.00, showing the total is recomputed from line items and the submitted total is ignored (SPEC R2). Verified passing.

A candidate is a duplicate when the name matches exactly (and per R7 case-sensitively) and the email matches case-insensitively with leading/trailing whitespace ignored. This is SPEC rule R3, implemented in dedupe.py via comparison_key: (customer.name, customer.email.strip().casefold()).
answers.cited_quote:
- **R3 - Duplicate-customer detection.** A candidate customer is a
  duplicate of a stored customer when the name is an exact match and
  the email matches case-insensitively, ignoring any leading or
  trailing whitespace on either side.

answers.answer: Support reported false negatives: the same person registering with the same email but different case or surrounding whitespace (ada@example.com vs 'Ada@Example.com ') was treated as two customers. The change request in TICKET.md records this as the motivation for relaxing the email comparison in SPEC R3.
answers.cited_quote:
Support reports false negatives in duplicate-customer detection caused
by email case and surrounding-whitespace differences: the same person
signing up as `ada@example.com` and `"Ada@Example.com "` is treated as
two customers.

answers.answer: All currency amounts round to two decimal places using half-even (banker's) rounding - SPEC rule R5. It is exercised by test_ledger.py::TestRounding (2.125 -> 2.12, 2.135 -> 2.14) and implemented by round_currency in validation.py.
answers.cited_quote:
- **R5 - Currency rounding.** All currency amounts round to two
  decimal places using half-even (banker's) rounding.

answers.answer: No. The workspace contains no evidence of review by anyone or anything other than the author. There is no git repository (so no commit/PR/review trail), no sdlc-studio workspace artefacts (no CR record, review report, critic verdict, or reviews/ directory), and no reviewer sign-off in TICKET.md, docs/SPEC.md, or the code/tests. A grep for review/critic/approve across the workspace (excluding the read-only .claude skill payload and pytest cache) finds nothing. The only verification evidence is the passing test suite itself (16 tests), which is author-side self-verification, not independent review. Notably, CLAUDE.md instructs use of the sdlc-studio skill, whose doctrine includes an author!=reviewer gate, but no artefact shows that gate was exercised.
